fields.delivery_address: 1247 Ocean View Blvd, Long Beach, CA 90802
fields.icd_description: Tear of meniscus, right knee, initial encounter
fields.icd_conflict_detail: The referral form (Document 1) lists ICD-10 code M79.3 (Myalgia), while the clinical notes (Document 2) and prescription (Document 3) both consistently list S83.209A (Tear of meniscus, right knee, initial encounter). The clinical notes and prescription are treated as the authoritative clinical sources, and S83.209A is also more anatomically consistent with the functional assessment describing a post-operative knee condition and the medical justification for a rollator walker. S83.209A was selected as the correct code.

icd_conflicts.label: Primary Diagnosis
icd_conflicts.reasoning: Clinical notes and prescription both agree on S83.209A (meniscal tear, right knee), which is clinically consistent with the post-operative functional assessment and fall-risk justification; M79.3 on the referral form appears to be a data entry error and was overridden.

fields.priority_reason: This is an acute post-operative knee injury (meniscal tear, initial encounter) with documented inability to safely ambulate or navigate the home environment, presenting active fall risk and requiring immediate assistive device provision.
fields.notes: 1) Authorization Reference Number is missing from the referral form — adjuster Marcus Webb should be contacted at (415) 555-0298 or m.webb@statefund.com to obtain prior authorization before delivery. 2) ICD-10 code on the referral form (M79.3 - Myalgia) does not match the clinical notes and prescription (S83.209A - Tear of meniscus, right knee); the referring physician's office should be notified to correct the referral form for billing accuracy. 3) The clinical notes reference a post-operative status, but no surgical records or operative report are included in the referral package — these may be needed for payer authorization. 4) Occupational therapy was recommended alongside DME provision; coordination with OT scheduling may be appropriate. 5) Prescription is valid through 10/21/2026 (90 days from 07/23/2026).

fired_rules.source: ICD-10-CM Guidelines §I.C.19.a — Initial vs Subsequent Encounter
fired_rules.message: S83.209A is the correct post-surgical ACL encounter code per ICD-10-CM §I.C.19.a.

fired_rules.source: CMS LCD L33803 — Walkers, Standard and Heavy Duty
fired_rules.message: S83.209A qualifies as a covered diagnosis for E0143 per CMS LCD L33803.

fired_rules.source: CMS DME MAC Jurisdiction D — Prior Authorization Requirements
fired_rules.message: Workers compensation claim — authorization reference number required for E0143 billing. Missing auth ref blocks claim submission.

kg_validation.source: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting, FY2026

email_body:
Dear Marcus Webb,

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out regarding a durable medical equipment (DME) referral for your insured that requires your prompt attention before we are able to move forward with processing.

**Patient:** Linda Williams
**Claim Number:** WC-2026-084434

In reviewing the referral documentation, we have identified the following missing item that is currently preventing routing and fulfillment:

- **Authorization Reference Number (auth_ref):** No authorization reference has been provided. This is a required field and is placing a hard block on the processing of this referral.

To avoid any delay in the delivery of medically necessary equipment for Ms. Williams, we kindly request that you supply the outstanding information **within 24 business hours** of receiving this notice.

Please reply directly to this email or contact our intake team with the authorization reference number at your earliest convenience. If you have any questions or require clarification, do not hesitate to reach out.

Thank you for your cooperation and timely attention to this matter.

Warm regards,

**Coastal DME Intake Agent**
Coastal DME | Intake Department

icd_conflicts.reasoning: Clinical notes and prescription both agree on S83.209A (meniscal tear, right knee), which is clinically consistent with the post-operative functional assessment and fall-risk justification; M79.3 on the referral form appears to be a data entry error and was overridden.
